name: Jarafuel, Muela de Juey, and el Castillico Route
name_original: Jarafuel a Muela de Juey al Castillico — circular
description: This difficult 28.5-mile mountain bike route near Jarafuel features savage climbs, speed descents, and stunning views of the Valencian mount
region_intro: Mountain biking around Jarafuel offers diverse terrain within the Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes region. The landscape features overlapping hills, mountains, and valleys, characterized by an extensive, pine-populated natural environment. Riders can expect varied topography, including deep gorges, rocky areas, and significant elevation changes, with the Júcar River providing scenic views. This area is known for its natural trails and challenging climbs. Best mountain bike trails around Jarafuel The most popular mountain bike trail is Jalance Route, a 19.8 miles (31.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses varied terrain, including technical sections and pine forests. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Hunde - Pico Palomera Loop, a moderate 14.3 miles (23.0 km) path. This route offers a blend of natural features and views, including ascents towards Pico Palomera. Local mountain bikers also love the Jarafuel, Muela de Juey, and el Castillico Route, a 28.5 miles (45.9 km) trail leading through varied topography and offering panoramic views, often completed in about 4 hours 44 minutes. Mountain biking around Jarafuel is defined by overlapping hills, extensive pine forests, and deep gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Jarafuel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 10 reviews. More than 160 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Jarafuel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Jarafuel?
A: Jarafuel offers a wide selection of mountain biking opportunities, with over 70 routes available. These range from easy paths suitable for beginners to challenging trails for experienced riders.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Jarafuel?
A: The terrain around Jarafuel is diverse, featuring overlapping hills, mountains, and valleys. You'll encounter extensive pine forests, deep gorges, and rocky, dry areas. Many routes include natural trails and challenging climbs, with significant elevation changes, especially towards peaks like Pico de Caroche.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails in Jarafuel?
A: Yes, Jarafuel has 5 easy mountain bike trails, perfect for those looking for a less strenuous ride or for families. Additionally, there are 51 moderate routes that offer a good balance of challenge and accessibility.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails in Jarafuel?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Jarafuel offers 16 difficult mountain bike trails. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1337723835/jarafuel-muela-de-juey-and-el-castillico-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarafuel, Muela de Juey, and el Castillico Route&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 45.9 km with significant elevation gain, often taking around 4 hours 44 minutes to complete. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Santa Cruz – El Caroche Summit loop from Teresa de Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt;, a 50.8 km loop with over 1300m of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Jarafuel?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Jarafuel are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1328874000/la-hunde-pico-palomera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hunde - Pico Palomera Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 23.0 km circular path that takes about 2 hours 5 minutes, offering a blend of natural features and views towards Pico Palomera.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Jarafuel's trails often pass by stunning natural features. You can encounter impressive gorges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2850716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cintos de la Cortada&lt;/a&gt; and enjoy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of the Júcar River&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also known for its springs and water features, including the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, where bathing is permitted.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or panoramic spots on the trails?
A: Absolutely. Many trails offer incredible views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6276620" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Caroche&lt;/a&gt;, reaching 1,126 meters, provides panoramic vistas in all directions. You can also find &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4374656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;spectacular views of the Hoz del Júcar&lt;/a&gt; along some routes, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5367776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Buitre&lt;/a&gt; is another notable viewpoint.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Jarafuel?
A: The komoot community highly rates mountain biking in Jarafuel, with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 10 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse topography, the extensive pine forests, and the challenging climbs that offer rewarding panoramic views.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can explore historical sites. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5367770" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remains of the hydroelectric power plant&lt;/a&gt; can be found along some routes. The nearby Chirel Castle also offers incredible views and is a popular spot for outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Jarafuel?
A: Jarafuel is an excellent destination for outdoor activities. The region's diverse topography and natural environment make it enjoyable across various seasons, though spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be hot, but the presence of springs and waterfalls like El Corbinet provides refreshing stops.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features or springs?
A: Yes, the area is known for its interesting springs and water features. You can find trails that lead to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which has a recreational area where bathing is allowed. The Júcar River also provides scenic views along many routes.
tips: Ancient Iberian settlement. Now it is a forest sighting point with an altitude of 1072m.
Jarafuel is located on the Castle Route in a beautiful wild nature. The ruins and castles have history with Templars, Romans, Moors etc. and are usually located on beautiful viewpoints.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Jarafuel, Muela de Juey, and el Castillico Route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in the village of Jarafuel. You can typically find parking within the village itself, near the main square or other public areas, to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult mountain bike route, suitable for fit riders or those with e-bikes. It features demanding 'savage climbs' and 'speed descents' with significant elevation changes, making it a pretty tough ride. Expect lots of singletrack, rocks, loose sections, and some sandy parts.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging. You'll encounter 'savage climbs' and 'speed descents,' extensive singletrack, and sections with abundant rocks and loose material. There are also sandy sections and old bridle paths, often winding through pine forests and along steep slopes. Some parts can be very loose and narrow, requiring careful navigation.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Jarafuel, Muela de Juey, and el Castillico Route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can enjoy picturesque views of the Valencian mountains, valleys, and rivers. Notable highlights include the Tollo Pillete Recreational and Camping Area, the Castillico Viewpoint offering stunning vistas, and the historic Fuente del Jarral. You'll also pass through a charming medieval village and see ancient castle ruins.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in this region. However, given the challenging nature of this mountain bike route with technical sections and potential exposure, ensure your dog is fit enough and always keep them on a leash for their safety and that of other trail users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride this route are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers in the Valencia region can be very hot, making the demanding climbs even more challenging. Winter riding is possible, but always check local weather conditions beforehand.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a special permit or have to pay an entrance fee to ride the Jarafuel, Muela de Juey, and el Castillico Route. It's freely accessible for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options near the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Jarafuel, a medieval village known for its traditional architecture. You should be able to find local cafes, restaurants, and potentially some accommodation options within the village to refuel and rest after your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking natural and historical scenery. You'll experience diverse landscapes, including overlapping hills, mountains, valleys, and rivers. Expect extensive pine forests, changing environments, and picturesque views of the Valencian mountains. The ancient castle ruins and the traditional architecture of Jarafuel also add to the visual interest.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily following the 'Jarafuel, Muela de Juey y el Castillico' trail for most of its length (96%), the route also briefly passes through sections of 'Senda los Burros'.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: No, this route is explicitly described as 'difficult' and 'pretty tough' with 'savage climbs' and technical terrain. It is not suitable for families or beginner mountain bikers. It's recommended for experienced and fit riders, or those using e-bikes to assist with the demanding ascents.

waypoints.name: Tollo Pillete Recreational and Camping Area
